title: intensely bitter brazilian san-marzano-tomato en-croute
description: a crispy, low-calorie friendly dish featuring san-marzano tomatoes, suitable for a solo dinner. this dish showcases the intense bitterness of the tomatoes and the crunch of the pastry crust.

ingredients.name: san-marzano tomatoes
ingredients.notes: heirloom tomatoes with a thick, meaty texture

instructions: preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C) with a baking stone inside, if available.
in a large bowl, combine the flour, salt, and a pinch of black pepper.
add the cubed butter to the flour mixture and use a pastry blender or your fingers to work it into the flour until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
add the grated parmesan cheese to the flour mixture and stir to combine.
add the beaten egg to the flour mixture and stir until the dough comes together in a ball.
knead the dough on a floured surface for about 5 minutes, until it becomes smooth and elastic.
divide the dough in half and roll out each half into a thin sheet.
place a sheet of parchment paper on a baking sheet and arrange the dough sheets on top.
combine the chopped onion, minced garlic, and chopped parsley in a small bowl.
spread the onion mixture evenly over the dough sheets, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
top the onion mixture with the halved san-marzano tomatoes.
fold the edges of the dough up over the filling, pressing gently to seal.
brush the tops of the en-croute with a little water and sprinkle with a pinch of salt and black pepper.
bake the en-croute for 25-30 minutes, or until golden brown and crispy.
remove the en-croute from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
